content.thinking:
Elasticsearch is running with TLS on HTTP. So the auth-checker probe that uses `http://` will get an empty reply (because ES expects HTTPS). 

The task says "ensure auth-checker becomes healthy." The auth-checker probe currently uses plain HTTP which won't work with Elasticsearch's TLS. I need to fix the auth-checker probe to use HTTPS (with `-k` flag).

But wait, let me re-read the task more carefully:
- "Rotate the elastic user password to match elastic-password-next"
- "Update the elastic-password secret to the new value"
- "Ensure auth-checker becomes healthy after the rotation"
The auth-checker is still initializing, but the core issue is that its probe uses HTTP while Elasticsearch requires HTTPS. I can either disable TLS on Elasticsearch or update the probe to use HTTPS with the insecure flag. Given the constraints around avoiding downtime and not using operators, updating the auth-checker deployment to use HTTPS is the cleanest path forward. I need to figure out whether the auth-checker is failing due to the HTTP vs HTTPS mismatch or because the password doesn't match. The current setup has `elastic-password` with the working password and `elastic-password-next` with the new one that hasn't been applied to ES yet. Since the auth-checker is using HTTP to probe HTTPS, it'll fail regardless of the password — but I should also check if the new `es-data-new` cluster might be configured differently and actually use HTTP instead.

content.thinking:
All Elasticsearch nodes use HTTPS. The auth-checker probe uses HTTP, which will always get an empty reply. I need to:

1. Rotate the elastic user password (via the Elasticsearch API)
2. Update the `elastic-password` secret
3. Fix the auth-checker deployment to use HTTPS in the probe

Let me look at the auth-checker deployment in more detail to understand how to patch it.

Actually let me re-read the task once more:
- "Rotate the elastic user password to match elastic-password-next."
- "Update the elastic-password secret to the new value."
- "Ensure auth-checker becomes healthy after the rotation."

The probe currently authenticates against Elasticsearch by making a curl request to verify the elastic user credentials. Since all ES nodes require HTTPS, I need to patch the auth-checker to use HTTPS with certificate verification disabled, which is much simpler than trying to expose an HTTP endpoint on Elasticsearch.

The key thing is getting the order right—I need to rotate the password in Elasticsearch first, then update the secret, and finally patch the auth-checker probe to use HTTPS. This avoids any window where the secret and actual password are out of sync.

content.text:
ES uses HTTPS but the auth-checker probe uses plain HTTP — I need to rotate the password, update the secret, and fix the probe to use HTTPS. Here's the plan:

1. Rotate the `elastic` user password in Elasticsearch
2. Update `elastic-password` secret to the new value
3. Patch auth-checker probe to use `https://` with `-k`

input.command:
NEW_PASS="elasticpass-elastic-password-next"
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch curl-test -- curl -s -k --max-time 15 \
  -u elastic:elasticpass \
  -X POST "https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/user/elastic/_password" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"password\":\"${NEW_PASS}\"}"

  -d '{"password":"elasticpass-elastic-password-next"}' 2>&1; echo " exit:$?"

content.text: Now patch the auth-checker probe to use HTTPS with `-k`:

input.description: Patch auth-checker readiness probe to use HTTPS

input.command:
PROBE_CMD='curl -s -k --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate | grep -q "\"username\":\"elastic\""'

kubectl patch deployment auth-checker -n elasticsearch --type=json \
  -p="$(python3 -c "
import json
patch = [{
  'op': 'replace',
  'path': '/spec/template/spec/containers/0/readinessProbe/exec/command',
  'value': ['/bin/sh', '-c', 'curl -s -k --max-time 5 -u elastic:\${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} https://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate | grep -q \\'\"username\":\"elastic\"\\'']
}]
print(json.dumps(patch))
")"
input.description: Patch auth-checker probe command using Python to handle quoting

input.command: kubectl rollout status deployment/auth-checker -n elasticsearch --timeout=120s
